verse_text: And being in Bethany at the house of Simon the leper, as He sat at the table, a woman came having an alabaster flask of very costly oil of spikenard. Then she broke the flask and poured it on His head.

links.context: Because of her fervent faith, Jesus promises perpetual public memory of this woman. There is no consensus among the Fathers as to her identity in relation to accounts of similar events in [[MRK.14:3]]-9; [[LUK.7:36]]-38; and [[JOH.12:1]]-8. Some Fathers say that there were three different women in these four accounts, others that there were only two.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: This prophetic verse was fulfilled by Mary of Bethany's anointing of the Lord with spikenard for His burial while He was at the table ([[MAT.26:6]], [[MAT.26:7]]; [[MRK.14:3]]; [[LUK.7:37]], [[LUK.7:38]]; Jn 12:3).
